freq.description: Applies activation checkpointing to every `freq` layers. Defaults to 1, which will is full activation checkpointing.
freq.title: Freq

pin_memory.description: Whether to pin the offloaded activations to CPU memory.
pin_memory.title: Pin Memory

max_inflight_activations.description: The maximum number of activations to keep in while offloading further. (More activations means smoother overlap, but more gpu memory usage)
max_inflight_activations.title: Max Inflight Activations

output_json.description: Path to write benchmark results as JSON. If not set, only prints to console.
output_json.title: Output Json

BufferConfig.description: Configures the buffer for the orchestrator.

seed.description: Random seed to use for the buffer. If set, the sampling from the buffer will be deterministic.
seed.title: Seed

env_ratios.description: Ratios for sampling from each environment. If None, samples uniformly across all available problems (not environments).
env_ratios.title: Env Ratios

easy_threshold.description: Threshold for easy difficulty classification. If average reward >= this threshold, mark as easy.
easy_threshold.title: Easy Threshold

hard_threshold.description: Threshold for hard difficulty classification. If average reward <= this threshold, mark as hard.
hard_threshold.title: Hard Threshold

easy_fraction.description: Fraction of easy problems to convert to normal when resuming or starting training. Only problems with difficulty 'normal' are sampled.
easy_fraction.title: Easy Fraction

hard_fraction.description: Fraction of hard problems to convert to normal when resuming or starting training. Only problems with difficulty 'normal' are sampled.
hard_fraction.title: Hard Fraction

online_difficulty_filtering.description: Whether to filter rollouts based on difficulty. If True, rollouts with average reward 0.0 or 1.0 are not added to the buffer.
online_difficulty_filtering.title: Online Difficulty Filtering

hash_keys.description: Keys to use for computing example hashes. Will be used to match examples from buffer checkpoints and determine buffer resume behavior.
hash_keys.title: Hash Keys

BufferConfig.title: BufferConfig

ClientConfig.description:
Configures the OAI client.

Supports two modes:
- Static mode (default): Uses fixed base_url list
- Elastic mode: Uses DNS-based service discovery via hostname

If elastic config is provided, base_url is ignored and servers are discovered dynamically.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds. By default, it is set to 1200 seconds.
timeout.title: Timeout

connect_timeout.description: TCP connect timeout in seconds for inference API requests.
connect_timeout.title: Connect Timeout

base_url.description: Base URLs to use for the OpenAI API. By default, it is set to a single server on localhost at port 8000 which matches the default local vLLM server configuration. If you specify more than one URL, the client will round-robin (chat) completion requests across all servers. Ignored if elastic config is provided.
base_url.title: Base Url

api_key_var.description: Name of environment variable containing the API key to use for the inference API. Will parse using `os.getenv(client_config.api_key_var)`. Can be set to an arbitrary string if the inference server is not protected by an API key. If multiple URLs are specified, the same API key will be used for all servers.
api_key_var.title: Api Key Var

headers.description: Headers to use for the OpenAI API. By default, it is set to an empty dictionary.
headers.title: Headers

skip_model_check.description: Whether to skip checking if the model is available in the inference pool. Useful for external APIs or API Keys that don't support the /models endpoint.
skip_model_check.title: Skip Model Check

dp_rank_count.description: Number of data-parallel ranks behind each base URL. When > 1, each URL is expanded into dp_rank_count logical clients, each pinned to a specific DP rank via the X-data-parallel-rank header. This ensures all requests within a multi-turn rollout hit the same DP engine, maximizing KV cache reuse. Auto-set from inference.data_parallel_size_local (or inference.parallel.dp) when using the RL entrypoint.
dp_rank_count.title: Dp Rank Count

elastic.description: Elastic inference pool configuration for DNS-based service discovery. If provided, base_url is ignored and inference servers are discovered dynamically via DNS.

router_url.description: URL of a vllm-router for load-aware inference routing. When set with elastic mode, inference requests go through the router while admin operations (weight updates, LoRA loading) still go directly to discovered pods.
router_url.title: Router Url

ClientConfig.title: ClientConfig

fullgraph.description: Whether to compile the transformer blocks with fullgraph.
fullgraph.title: Fullgraph

ConstantSchedulerConfig.description: Configuration for constant learning rate scheduler.
ConstantSchedulerConfig.title: ConstantSchedulerConfig

CosineSchedulerConfig.description: Configuration for cosine learning rate scheduler.

warmup_steps.description: Number of warmup steps for the learning rate scheduler.
warmup_steps.title: Warmup Steps

min_lr.description: Minimum learning rate to converge to.
min_lr.title: Min Lr

CosineSchedulerConfig.title: CosineSchedulerConfig

CustomAdvantageConfig.description: Config for a custom external advantage function.

import_path.description: Import path to the advantage function (e.g., 'my_module.my_advantage')
import_path.title: Import Path

kwargs.description: Kwargs to pass to the advantage function
kwargs.title: Kwargs

CustomAdvantageConfig.title: CustomAdvantageConfig

CustomLossConfig.description: Config for a custom external loss function.

import_path.description: Import path to the loss function (e.g., 'my_module.my_loss')
import_path.title: Import Path

kwargs.description: Kwargs to pass to the loss function
kwargs.title: Kwargs

CustomLossConfig.title: CustomLossConfig

DataLoaderConfig.description: Configures the data loader used for training.

fake.description: Whether to use a fake data loader.

DataLoaderConfig.title: DataLoaderConfig

DebugModelConfig.description: Debugging feature around model and distributed training.

num_layers.description: The number of layers in the model.
num_layers.title: Num Layers

random_init.description: Whether to random initialize the model.
random_init.title: Random Init

DebugModelConfig.title: DebugModelConfig

length_shaping_alpha.description: Penalty coefficient for Group Relative Reward Rescaling (GR³). Recommended value: 0.33
length_shaping_alpha.title: Length Shaping Alpha

ipo_mask_low.description: The low threshold for masking tokens.
ipo_mask_low.title: Ipo Mask Low

ipo_mask_high.description: The high threshold for masking tokens.
ipo_mask_high.title: Ipo Mask High

ElasticConfig.description:
Configures elastic inference pool with DNS-based service discovery.

Works with any DNS hostname that resolves to multiple IP addresses.

hostname.description: DNS hostname that resolves to inference server IPs.
hostname.title: Hostname

port.description: Port that inference servers listen on.
port.title: Port

sync_interval.description: Interval in seconds between server discovery checks.
sync_interval.title: Sync Interval

ElasticConfig.title: ElasticConfig

id.description: ID of the environment to use.
id.title: Id

args.description: Arguments to pass to the environment.
args.title: Args

name.description: Name of the environment to use.
name.title: Name

address.description: Address of the environment server. If None, will spawn an environment server in a subprocess automatically.If given, will try to connect an environment client to the environment server at this address.
address.title: Address

extra_env_kwargs.description: Extra kwargs passed to an env (e.g. seq_len, score_rollouts). This field is auto-populated with the seq_len, and score_rollouts for training envs on the orchestrator. It is generally NOT recommended for this field to be overriden by the user. It's main use case is to match the extra_env_kwargs when running an env in an isolated environment server.
extra_env_kwargs.title: Extra Env Kwargs

max_retries.description: Maximum number of times the environment will retry a failed rollout.
max_retries.title: Max Retries

sampling.description: Shared sampling configuration for evals; can differ from training sampling.

num_examples.description: Number of examples to evaluate per environment.
num_examples.title: Num Examples

rollouts_per_example.description: Number of samples to generate per example for each environment.
rollouts_per_example.title: Rollouts Per Example

interval.description: Interval at which to evaluate the model.
interval.title: Interval

eval_base_model.description: Whether to evaluate the base model we are training on.
eval_base_model.title: Eval Base Model

skip_eval_on_resume.description: If True and resuming the orchestrator from a checkpoint, skip the (potentially redundant) online eval that would otherwise run immediately at the resumed checkpoint step.
skip_eval_on_resume.title: Skip Eval On Resume

cancel_inflight_rollouts_on_eval.description: Whether to cancel in-flight training rollouts before starting online evals. This is useful to avoid congestion (e.g. do not have training + eval rollouts happening at the same time) but leads to slower training steps as rollouts get cancelled and the pipeline has to fill up after each eval
cancel_inflight_rollouts_on_eval.title: Cancel Inflight Rollouts On Eval

id.description: ID of the environment to use.
id.title: Id

args.description: Arguments to pass to the environment.
args.title: Args

name.description: Name of the environment to use.
name.title: Name

address.description: Address of the environment server. If None, will spawn an environment server in a subprocess automatically.If given, will try to connect an environment client to the environment server at this address.
address.title: Address

extra_env_kwargs.description: Extra kwargs passed to an env (e.g. seq_len, score_rollouts). This field is auto-populated with the seq_len, and score_rollouts for training envs on the orchestrator. It is generally NOT recommended for this field to be overriden by the user. It's main use case is to match the extra_env_kwargs when running an env in an isolated environment server.
extra_env_kwargs.title: Extra Env Kwargs

max_retries.description: Maximum number of times the environment will retry a failed rollout.
max_retries.title: Max Retries

num_examples.description: Number of examples to evaluate per environment. If not set, will use 'num_examples' from main config.
num_examples.title: Num Examples

rollouts_per_example.description: Number of samples to generate per example for each environment. If not set, will use 'rollouts_per_example' from main config.
rollouts_per_example.title: Rollouts Per Example

skip_first.description: Number of examples to skip from the beginning of the dataset.
skip_first.title: Skip First

EvalSamplingConfig.description: Configures how tokens are sampled from the model for evaluation. Largely follows the vLLM sampling parameters.

temperature.description: Scales the output probability distribution. Lower values => more deterministic, higher values => more random. If 0, will sample greedily. Defaults to None, which means we fall back to the inference server's default value.
temperature.title: Temperature

repetition_penalty.description: Penalty for repeating tokens. Values > 1.0 discourage repetition, values < 1.0 encourage repetition, and 1.0 means no penalty. Defaults to None, which means we fall back to the inference server's default value.
repetition_penalty.title: Repetition Penalty

top_p.description: Cumulative probability of the top tokens to consider. If 1, all tokens are considered. Defaults to None, which means we fall back to the inference server's default value.
top_p.title: Top P

top_k.description: Number of top tokens to consider. If -1, all tokens are considered. Defaults to None, which means we fall back to the inference server's default value.
top_k.title: Top K

min_p.description: Minimum probability for a token to be considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token. If 0, all tokens are considered. Defaults to None, which means we fall back to the inference server's default value.
min_p.title: Min P

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of output tokens to generate per turn. If None, will generate until maximum context length or EOS token is hit.
max_tokens.title: Max Tokens

min_tokens.description: Minimum number of output tokens to generate per sequence. Defaults to None, which means we fall back to the inference server's default value.
min_tokens.title: Min Tokens

reasoning_effort.description: Constrains effort on reasoning for reasoning models. Currently supported values are minimal, low, medium, and high. Defaults to None, which means we fall back to the inference server's default value.
reasoning_effort.title: Reasoning Effort

seed.description: Random seed to use for sampling. If None, no seeding is used. Defaults to None, which means we fall back to the inference server's default value.
seed.title: Seed

extra_body.description: Extra body to use for the OpenAI API. By default, it is set to an empty dictionary.
extra_body.title: Extra Body

EvalSamplingConfig.title: EvalSamplingConfig

FakeDataLoaderConfig.description: Configures a fake data loader sampling random micro batches for debugging.

generate_samples.description: Whether to generate separate samples and pack them into a single micro batch.
generate_samples.title: Generate Samples

FakeDataLoaderConfig.title: FakeDataLoaderConfig

FileSystemTransportConfig.description: Configures filesystem-based transport for training examples.
FileSystemTransportConfig.title: FileSystemTransportConfig

GibberishFilterConfig.description: Flags rare tokens generated at high entropy (Section 5.2, https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.02387).

enforce.description: If True, mask detected rollouts so they don't contribute to training. If False, only track detection metrics.
enforce.title: Enforce

token_id_threshold.description: Token IDs above this are candidates for gibberish. BPE tokens are sorted by merge order.
token_id_threshold.title: Token Id Threshold

logprob_offset.description: Offset from uniform distribution logprob. Threshold = -log(vocab_size) - logprob_offset.
logprob_offset.title: Logprob Offset

GibberishFilterConfig.title: GibberishFilterConfig

url.description: The URL to send the heartbeat to.
url.title: Url

enable_lora.description: Whether to enable LORA. Passed to vLLM as `--enable-lora`
enable_lora.title: Enable Lora

max_loras.description: The maximum number of LoRAs to use. Passed to vLLM as `--max-loras`
max_loras.title: Max Loras

max_cpu_loras.description: The maximum number of LoRAs to use on CPU. Passed to vLLM as `--max-cpu-loras`
max_cpu_loras.title: Max Cpu Loras

max_lora_rank.description: The maximum LoRA rank to use. Passed to vLLM as `--max-lora-rank`
max_lora_rank.title: Max Lora Rank

enable_prefix_caching.description: Whether to enable prefix caching. Passed to vLLM as `--enable-prefix-caching`
enable_prefix_caching.title: Enable Prefix Caching

gpu_memory_utilization.description: The GPU memory utilization to use. Passed to vLLM as `--gpu-memory-utilization`
gpu_memory_utilization.title: Gpu Memory Utilization

api_server_count.description: The number of API servers to use. Passed to vLLM as `--api-server-count`
api_server_count.title: Api Server Count

data_parallel_size_local.description: Number of data parallel replicas to run on this node. Passed to vLLM as `--data-parallel-size-local`.
data_parallel_size_local.title: Data Parallel Size Local

data_parallel_rpc_port.description: RPC port for data parallel communication. Passed to vLLM as `--data-parallel-rpc-port`.
data_parallel_rpc_port.title: Data Parallel Rpc Port

seed.description: Seed the inference components. Passed to vLLM as `--seed`
seed.title: Seed

enable_expert_parallel.description: Enable expert parallelism for MoE models. Passed to vLLM as `--enable-expert-parallel`.
enable_expert_parallel.title: Enable Expert Parallel

all2all_backend.description: All-to-all backend for expert parallel communication. Passed to vLLM as `--all2all-backend`.
all2all_backend.title: All2All Backend

enable_eplb.description: Enable expert parallel load balancer (EPLB). Passed to vLLM as `--enable-eplb`.
enable_eplb.title: Enable Eplb

enable_return_routed_experts.description: Whether to enable return routed experts. Passed to vLLM as `--enable-return-routed-experts`
enable_return_routed_experts.title: Enable Return Routed Experts

vllm_extra.description: Extra arguments to pass to vLLM. These are applied as attributes on the vLLM namespace after config translation.
vllm_extra.title: Vllm Extra

slurm.description: SLURM configuration. If set, the run will be submitted as a SLURM job instead of running locally.

output_dir.description: Directory for SLURM logs and generated scripts.
output_dir.title: Output Dir

dry_run.description: Only validate and dump resolved configs and exit early.
dry_run.title: Dry Run

LinearSchedulerConfig.description: Configuration for linear learning rate scheduler.

warmup_steps.description: Number of warmup steps for the learning rate scheduler.
warmup_steps.title: Warmup Steps

decay_steps.description: Number of steps to decay the learning rate during the final portion of training.
decay_steps.title: Decay Steps

min_lr.description: Minimum learning rate to converge to.
min_lr.title: Min Lr

LinearSchedulerConfig.title: LinearSchedulerConfig

level.description: Logging level for the process. Will determine the logging verbosity and format.
level.title: Level

vf_level.description: Logging level for the verifiers package. Will determine the logging verbosity and format.
vf_level.title: Vf Level

file.description: Whether to log to a file. If True, will log to a file in the output directory.
file.title: File

env_worker_logs.description: Whether env workers log to files. If True, workers write to logs/env_workers/{env_name}.log.
env_worker_logs.title: Env Worker Logs

log_data.description: Whether to log the first data sample to the logger.
log_data.title: Log Data

json_logging.description: Emit JSON logs (newline-delimited) for log aggregation (Loki, Grafana, etc.).
json_logging.title: Json Logging

LogExtrasConfig.description: Configures extra logging for monitoring platforms.

distributions.description: Whether to log distributions (like rewards, advantages, etc.).
distributions.title: Distributions

interval.description: Step interval at which to log extras.
interval.title: Interval

sample_ratio.description: Fraction of rollouts to log per step (0.0–1.0). When set, the effective sample cap is len(rollouts) * sample_ratio. 1.0 = all rollouts, 0.5 = half, 0.0 = none. None (default)
sample_ratio.title: Sample Ratio

LogExtrasConfig.title: LogExtrasConfig

MetricsServerConfig.description: Configures the Prometheus metrics server for trainer observability.

port.description: Port to expose metrics and health endpoints. Defaults to 8000.
port.title: Port

host.description: Host to bind the server to. Defaults to 0.0.0.0.
host.title: Host

MetricsServerConfig.title: MetricsServerConfig

num_infer_nodes.description: Number of inference nodes. Set to 0 to skip inference and orchestrator (requires fake data).
num_infer_nodes.title: Num Infer Nodes

nodes_per_fsdp_group.description: Number of training nodes per FSDP island. Auto-sets trainer.dp_replicate = num_train_nodes / nodes_per_fsdp_group.
nodes_per_fsdp_group.title: Nodes Per Fsdp Group

MultiNodeInferenceDeploymentConfig.description: Configures a multi-node inference deployment. Each node runs an independent vLLM replica.
MultiNodeInferenceDeploymentConfig.title: MultiNodeInferenceDeploymentConfig

mu.description: Momentum factor for the Muon algorithm.
mu.title: Mu

betas1.description: Beta1 for the AdamW/Lion sub-optimizer used on non-Muon params.
betas1.title: Betas1

betas2.description: Beta2 for the AdamW/Lion sub-optimizer used on non-Muon params.
betas2.title: Betas2

OptimizerConfig.description: Per-run optimizer configuration for multi-run training.
OptimizerConfig.title: OptimizerConfig

OrchestratorConfig.description: Configures the orchestrator for RL training.

teacher_model.description: The teacher model configuration for computing teacher logprobs (e.g. for distillation). If provided, teacher logprobs will be computed using the specified model. If None, no teacher model will be used.

teacher_rollout_model.description: Optional external teacher model used for rollout generation. When set, rollouts are generated from this endpoint/model instead of the student inference server.

output_dir.description: Directory to write outputs to. Will be populated with checkpoints, weights, rollouts and logs as subdirectories. Should be set to a persistent directory with enough disk space. This value should be distinct across experiments running on a single node. See the README for more details.
output_dir.title: Output Dir

max_concurrent.description: Maximum number of concurrent rollouts to generate and score per-environment. If None, will not limit concurrency.
max_concurrent.title: Max Concurrent

tasks_per_minute.description: Rate limit for tasks per environment worker, in tasks per minute. Recommended for sandbox-backed environments to prevent sandbox-not-ready errors during autoscaling. When set to None, no rate limiting is applied. Note: with multiple workers, the effective total rate equals workers × this value.
tasks_per_minute.title: Tasks Per Minute

batch_size.description: Number of samples to train on per step (rollout-based batching). Set this OR token_batch_size.
batch_size.title: Batch Size

token_batch_size.description: Number of tokens to train on per step (token-based batching). Set this OR batch_size.
token_batch_size.title: Token Batch Size

oversampling_factor.description: Rollout-mode batching only. Multiplier used to derive max_inflight_rollouts from batch_size when max_inflight_rollouts is unset.
oversampling_factor.title: Oversampling Factor

max_inflight_rollouts.description: Maximum number of rollouts to keep in-flight. Required for token-based batching. If batch_size is set and this is unset, defaults to batch_size * oversampling_factor (or batch_size when oversampling_factor is unset).
max_inflight_rollouts.title: Max Inflight Rollouts

rollouts_per_example.description: Number of output sequences to return per example during training.
rollouts_per_example.title: Rollouts Per Example

seq_len.description: Sequence length to use for training. If a sample is shorter than this, it will be padded. If a sequence is longer than this, it will be truncated.
seq_len.title: Seq Len

num_train_workers.description: Number of training workers to use for training.
num_train_workers.title: Num Train Workers

max_steps.description: Maximum number of training steps to run. If None, will run indefinitely.
max_steps.title: Max Steps

max_off_policy_steps.description: Maximum number of policies that are allowed to generate a single rollout. Rollouts that are generated from more than `max_off_policy_steps` steps ahead of training will be discarded. Higher values yield better throughput, but lead to more off-policyness in training.
max_off_policy_steps.title: Max Off Policy Steps

max_async_level.description: Maximum number of steps the inference can be ahead of training. If 0, will degenerate to synchronous on-policy RL. If >=1, training and inference will be overlapped.
max_async_level.title: Max Async Level

strict_async_level.description: Whether to strictly enforce the max async level. If True, will always ensure that the policy used for generating rollouts is exactly `max_async_level` steps ahead of training. If False, any policy that is at most `max_async_level` steps ahead of training is allowed, i.e. we always use the latest available policy.
strict_async_level.title: Strict Async Level

bench.description: Whether to run in benchmark mode. It will automatically set the maximum number of steps to run to 5, max async level to ~infinity and disable W&B.
bench.title: Bench

heartbeat.description: The heartbeat config for monitoring training progress.

use_token_client.description: Whether to use the token-in-token-out (TITO) client for training across all environments. WARNING: Only use this if your environment has a linear history and the chat template has the extension property (i.e. no tokens are ever removed or inserted by the chat template)
use_token_client.title: Use Token Client

OrchestratorConfig.title: OrchestratorConfig

ParallelConfig.description: Configures multi-node and multi-GPU setups through different types of parallelism (TP, DP, PP).

tp.description: The tensor parallel size. It is passed to vLLM as `--tensor-parallel-size`
tp.title: Tp

dp.description: The data parallel size. It is passed to vLLM as `--data-parallel-size`
dp.title: Dp

ParallelConfig.title: ParallelConfig

PrimeMonitorConfig.description: Configures logging to Prime Intellect API.

base_url.description: The base URL for Prime Intellect monitoring API.
base_url.title: Base Url

api_key_var.description: Name of environment variable containing the API key for Prime Intellect API. Will parse using `os.getenv(config.api_key_var)`.
api_key_var.title: Api Key Var

log_extras.description: Configuration for logging extras. If None, no extras are logged.

run_name.description: Name for the run shown on the platform. Defaults to the W&B run name if set, otherwise auto-generated by the platform.
run_name.title: Run Name

team_id.description: Team ID to associate the run with.
team_id.title: Team Id

frontend_url.description: Frontend base URL used for the dashboard link shown after registration. Defaults to the Prime CLI frontend URL when unset.
frontend_url.title: Frontend Url

PrimeMonitorConfig.title: PrimeMonitorConfig

RepetitionFilterConfig.description:
Flags rollouts where the model gets stuck in a repetition loop, emitting high-confidence tokens
for an extended stretch. A rollout is flagged when `window` consecutive tokens are each sampled
with probability above `prob_threshold`. (Section 3.2, https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.13585)

enforce.description: If True, mask detected rollouts so they don't contribute to training. If False, only track detection metrics.
enforce.title: Enforce

window.description: Number of consecutive high-probability steps before flagging.
window.title: Window

prob_threshold.description: Tokens sampled with probability above this are considered repetitive. Consecutive such tokens count toward the window.
prob_threshold.title: Prob Threshold

RepetitionFilterConfig.title: RepetitionFilterConfig

SFTLossConfig.description: Config for SFT-style masked negative log-likelihood loss.
SFTLossConfig.title: SFTLossConfig

SamplingConfig.description: Configures how tokens are sampled from the model for training. Largely follows the vLLM sampling parameters.

temperature.description: Constant temperature for sampling. Defaults to 1.0 if neither this nor temp_scheduler is set. Cannot be set together with temp_scheduler.
temperature.title: Temperature

temp_scheduler.description: Temperature schedule over training steps. Set this OR temperature, not both.

repetition_penalty.description: Penalty for repeating tokens. Values > 1.0 discourage repetition, values < 1.0 encourage repetition, and 1.0 means no penalty.
repetition_penalty.title: Repetition Penalty

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of output tokens to generate per turn. If None, will generate until maximum context length or EOS token is hit.
max_tokens.title: Max Tokens

min_tokens.description: Minimum number of output tokens to generate per sequence.
min_tokens.title: Min Tokens

seed.description: Random seed to use for sampling. If None, no seeding is used.
seed.title: Seed

extra_body.description: Extra body to pass with each request to the inference server. By default, it is set to an empty dictionary.
extra_body.title: Extra Body

SamplingConfig.title: SamplingConfig

output_dir.description: Override directory for checkpoints and weights. When set, checkpoints and weight snapshots are written here instead of under the trainer output_dir.
output_dir.title: Output Dir

interval.description: The interval at which to save checkpoints.
interval.title: Interval

resume_step.description: The step to resume from. If None, will not resume from a checkpoint.
resume_step.title: Resume Step

keep_last.description: Keep at most this many recent step checkpoints on disk. If None, never clean old checkpoints based on recency.
keep_last.title: Keep Last

keep_interval.description: Keep checkpoints at every N steps permanently (e.g., keep_interval=100 keeps step 100, 200, ...). If None, no interval-based keeping.
keep_interval.title: Keep Interval

file.description: Whether to log to a file.
file.title: File

json_logging.description: Emit JSON logs (newline-delimited) for log aggregation (Loki, Grafana, etc.).
json_logging.title: Json Logging

name.description: The name of the model to use.
name.title: Name

name.description: The W&B run name to use.
name.title: Name

offline.description: Whether to run W&B in offline mode.
offline.title: Offline

shared.description: Use shared W&B mode to log trainer and orchestrator metrics to a single run. Requires wandb SDK >= 0.19.9. Incompatible with offline mode.
shared.title: Shared

type.description: The type of weight broadcast to use.
type.title: Type

port.description: The port to use for NCCL weight broadcast.
port.title: Port

timeout.description: The timeout in seconds for NCCL weight broadcast.
timeout.title: Timeout

quantize_in_weight_transfer.description: Use kernel-format FP8 quantized NCCL transfer for weight updates. When disabled, uses default HF checkpoint-format transfer.
quantize_in_weight_transfer.title: Quantize In Weight Transfer

project_dir.description: Path to the project root. Used to source .env, activate .venv, and run uv sync.
project_dir.title: Project Dir

template_path.description: The path to the SLURM template file. If None, will use the default single-node/multi-node template.
template_path.title: Template Path

partition.description: The SLURM partition to use. Will be passed as #SBATCH --partition.
partition.title: Partition

nodelist.description: Comma-separated list of specific nodes to run on. Passed as #SBATCH --nodelist.
nodelist.title: Nodelist

exclude.description: Comma-separated list of nodes to exclude. Passed as #SBATCH --exclude.
exclude.title: Exclude

account.description: SLURM account to charge. Passed as #SBATCH --account.
account.title: Account

pre_run_command.description: Shell command to run on the head node before starting the job. Runs after cd into project dir, .env sourcing, and venv activation. Useful for cleanup routines like 'sudo pkill -f vllm'. To run on all nodes, wrap with srun: 'srun bash -c "pkill -f vllm || true"'.
pre_run_command.title: Pre Run Command

TeacherModelConfig.description: Configures the teacher model for computing teacher logprobs (e.g. for distillation).

client.description: The OAI client configuration for the teacher model.

model.description: The model configuration for the teacher model.

TeacherModelConfig.title: TeacherModelConfig

TeacherRolloutModelConfig.description: Configures an external teacher model used to generate rollout text.

client.description: The OAI client configuration for rollout generation.

model.description: The model configuration for rollout generation.

TeacherRolloutModelConfig.title: TeacherRolloutModelConfig

TemperatureSchedulerConfig.description: Configures temperature scheduling over training steps. Use this OR sampling.temperature, not both.

type.description: Schedule shape. Linear interpolates linearly; cosine uses smooth, monotonic curve.
type.title: Type

total_steps.description: Number of steps to reach end_temperature. Defaults to orchestrator max_steps if None.
total_steps.title: Total Steps

TemperatureSchedulerConfig.title: TemperatureSchedulerConfig

name.description: The name or path of the tokenizer to use. If None, will use the model's default tokenizer.
name.title: Name

trust_remote_code.description: Whether to trust remote code for tokenizer initialization. If None, will use the model's default trust remote code setting.
trust_remote_code.title: Trust Remote Code

chat_template.description: The chat template to use for the tokenizer. If None, will use the tokenizer's default chat template.
chat_template.title: Chat Template

output_dir.description: Directory to write outputs to. Will be populated with checkpoints, weights, rollouts and logs as subdirectories. Should be set to a persistent directory with enough disk space. This value should be distinct across experiments running on a single node. See the README for more details.
output_dir.title: Output Dir

max_steps.description: Maximum number of steps to run training for. If None, will run indefinitely.
max_steps.title: Max Steps

max_async_level.description: Maximum number of steps that inference can be ahead of training. Determines how 'off-policy' the inference engines can be. Higher values yield better throughput through async execution, but may yield lower performance. If 0, will be fully synchronous.
max_async_level.title: Max Async Level

enable_router_replay.description: Whether to enable router replay. If True, will return routed experts in the batch. This is only supported if `enable_return_routed_experts=True` in the inference config or pass `--enable-return-routed-experts` to vLLM server. This is only supported for custom models.
enable_router_replay.title: Enable Router Replay

memory_profiler_path.description: Path to write memory profile to.
memory_profiler_path.title: Memory Profiler Path

bench.description: Whether to run in benchmark mode. It will automatically set the maximum number of steps to run to 4 and use fake data.

trace_path.description: Path to write pytorch profiler trace to.
trace_path.title: Trace Path

dist_timeout_seconds.description: Timeout in seconds for torch distributed ops. Defaults to 600 seconds.
dist_timeout_seconds.title: Dist Timeout Seconds

heartbeat.description: The heartbeat config for monitoring training progress.

metrics_server.description: Prometheus metrics server config. If set, exposes /metrics endpoint for scraping.

max_concurrent_runs.description: The maximum number of concurrent runs to allow. If 1, then only one run will be allowed at a time.
max_concurrent_runs.title: Max Concurrent Runs

ValConfig.description: Configures the validation of the model.

num_examples.description: Number of examples to use for validation. If -1, will use all examples.
num_examples.title: Num Examples

rollouts_per_example.description: Number of samples to generate per example for validation.
rollouts_per_example.title: Rollouts Per Example

interval.description: Interval at which to validate the model.
interval.title: Interval

ValConfig.title: ValConfig

VerificationConfig.description: Configures rollout verification and rubric scoring.

enabled.description: Whether to verify training rollouts using the environment rubric. If False, rewards are always set to 0.
enabled.title: Enabled

VerificationConfig.title: VerificationConfig

WandbConfig.description: Configures logging to Weights and Biases.

project.description: The W&B project to log to.
project.title: Project

name.description: The W&B name to to use for logging.
name.title: Name

offline.description: Whether to run W&B in offline mode.
offline.title: Offline

WandbConfig.title: WandbConfig

WandbWithExtrasConfig.description: Configures logging to Weights and Biases with extras.

project.description: The W&B project to log to.
project.title: Project

name.description: The W&B name to to use for logging.
name.title: Name

offline.description: Whether to run W&B in offline mode.
offline.title: Offline

log_extras.description: Configuration for logging extras. If None, no extras are logged.

WandbWithExtrasConfig.title: WandbWithExtrasConfig

type.description: The type of weight broadcast to use.
type.title: Type

save_sharded.description: Whether to save the weight checkpoint in sharded format.
save_sharded.title: Save Sharded

save_format.description: The format to save the weight checkpoint in.
save_format.title: Save Format

save_adapter_separately.description: Whether to save LoRA adapters separately before merging into full model weights.
save_adapter_separately.title: Save Adapter Separately

ZMQTransportConfig.description: Configures ZMQ-based transport for training examples.

host.description: The host address for ZMQ transport.
host.title: Host

port.description: The base port for ZMQ transport.
port.title: Port

hwm.description: High water mark (max messages in queue) for ZMQ sockets.
hwm.title: Hwm

ZMQTransportConfig.title: ZMQTransportConfig

prime_rl__configs__inference__ModelConfi.description: Configures the inference model. Most arguments are passed directly to the vLLM LLM class (https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/api/vllm.LLM.html).

name.description: Name or path of the HF model to use.
name.title: Name

trust_remote_code.description: Whether to trust remote code. Passed to vLLM engine init
trust_remote_code.title: Trust Remote Code

dtype.description: Data type for model weights and activations. If 'auto' will use FP16 precision for FP32 and FP16 models, and BF16 precision for BF16 models. Passed to vLLM as `--dtype`
dtype.title: Dtype

max_model_len.description: Maximum model context length. If None, will use the maximum context length from model config. Passed to vLLM as `--max-model-len`
max_model_len.title: Max Model Len

enforce_eager.description: Whether to enforce eager mode. If False, will use PyTorch eager and cuda graphs in hybrid for maximal performance. Passed to vLLM as `--enforce-eager`
enforce_eager.title: Enforce Eager

tool_call_parser.description: The tool call parser to use. Passed to vLLM as `--tool-call-parser`. Set to "auto" to infer from the model name.
tool_call_parser.title: Tool Call Parser

reasoning_parser.description: Parser for extracting reasoning content from model outputs. Passed to vLLM as `--reasoning-parser`. Setting this enables reasoning mode.
reasoning_parser.title: Reasoning Parser

rope_scaling.description: RoPE scaling configuration as a dict. For YaRN, use: {rope_type="yarn", factor=4.0, original_max_position_embeddings=32768} or. Passed to vLLM as `--rope-scaling`.
rope_scaling.title: Rope Scaling

prime_rl__configs__inference__ModelConfi.title: ModelConfig

interval.description: Interval at which to save the checkpoint.
interval.title: Interval

resume_step.description: Step to resume orchestrator from. If None, will start from scratch. If -1, will restart from latest checkpoint available.
resume_step.title: Resume Step

wait_for_weights_timeout.description: When resuming, wait up to this many seconds for the weight directory to appear. Useful when the orchestrator restarts while the trainer is still saving weights. If None (default), fail immediately if weights are not found.
wait_for_weights_timeout.title: Wait For Weights Timeout

keep_last.description: Keep at most this many recent step checkpoints on disk. If None, never clean old checkpoints based on recency.
keep_last.title: Keep Last

keep_interval.description: Keep checkpoints at every N steps permanently (e.g., keep_interval=100 keeps step 100, 200, ...). If None, no interval-based keeping.
keep_interval.title: Keep Interval

skip_progress.description: Whether to skip loading the progress from checkpoint.
skip_progress.title: Skip Progress

skip_buffer.description: Whether to skip loading the buffer from checkpoint.
skip_buffer.title: Skip Buffer

prime_rl__configs__orchestrator__LoRACon.description: Per-run LoRA configuration for multi-run training.

name.description: Name of the LoRA adapter. If None, auto-generated from rank and alpha.
name.title: Name

rank.description: LoRA rank for this run. Must be <= trainer's max rank. If None, uses trainer's rank.
rank.title: Rank

alpha.description: LoRA alpha for this run. If None, uses trainer's alpha.
alpha.title: Alpha

prime_rl__configs__orchestrator__LoRACon.title: LoRAConfig

prime_rl__configs__orchestrator__ModelCo.description: Extended model configuration with per-run LoRA settings.

name.description: Name or path of the HF model to use.
name.title: Name

trust_remote_code.description: Whether to trust remote code for tokenizer initialization.
trust_remote_code.title: Trust Remote Code

lora.description: LoRA configuration. If None, LoRA is not used.

prime_rl__configs__orchestrator__ModelCo.title: ModelConfig

host.description: The host to use for the NCCL broadcast.
host.title: Host

port.description: The port to use for the NCCL broadcast.
port.title: Port

timeout.description: The timeout in seconds to use for the NCCL broadcast.
timeout.title: Timeout

quantize_in_weight_transfer.description: Use kernel-format FP8 quantized NCCL transfer for weight updates.
quantize_in_weight_transfer.title: Quantize In Weight Transfer

prime_rl__configs__trainer__CheckpointCo.description: Configures checkpointing the full model, optimizer and training state for resuming training.

output_dir.description: Override directory for checkpoints and weights. When set, checkpoints and weight snapshots are written here instead of under the trainer output_dir. Useful for writing large checkpoints to a separate storage volume.
output_dir.title: Output Dir

interval.description: Interval at which to save the training checkpoint. If None, will only checkpoint at the end of training.
interval.title: Interval

weights_only.description: When true, only save weight checkpoints (no optimizer/scheduler state). Much faster and smaller than full checkpoints, but cannot resume training.
weights_only.title: Weights Only

resume_step.description: Step to resume training from. If None, will start from scratch. If -1, will restart from latest checkpoint available.
resume_step.title: Resume Step

keep_last.description: Keep at most this many recent step checkpoints on disk. If None, never clean old checkpoints based on recency.
keep_last.title: Keep Last

keep_interval.description: Keep checkpoints at every N steps permanently (e.g., keep_interval=100 keeps step 100, 200, ...). If None, no interval-based keeping.
keep_interval.title: Keep Interval

skip_progress.description: Whether to skip loading the progress from checkpoint.
skip_progress.title: Skip Progress

skip_scheduler.description: Whether to skip loading the scheduler from checkpoint.
skip_scheduler.title: Skip Scheduler

skip_dataloader.description: Whether to skip loading the dataloader from checkpoint.
skip_dataloader.title: Skip Dataloader

prime_rl__configs__trainer__CheckpointCo.title: CheckpointConfig

save_sharded.description: Whether to save the weight checkpoint in sharded format.
save_sharded.title: Save Sharded

save_format.description: The format to save the weight checkpoint in.
save_format.title: Save Format

rank.description: Rank of the low-rank decomposition matrices.
rank.title: Rank

target_modules.description: Module names or regex patterns for modules to apply LoRA to. Simple names (e.g., 'q_proj') match any component in the module path. Regex patterns match anywhere in the name.
target_modules.title: Target Modules

modules_to_save.description: Module names or regex patterns for modules to keep fully trainable (not freeze). Simple names match any component in the module path. Regex patterns match anywhere in the name.
modules_to_save.title: Modules To Save

name.description: Name or path of the HF model to use.
name.title: Name

trust_remote_code.description: Whether to trust remote code for tokenizer initialization.
trust_remote_code.title: Trust Remote Code

seq_len.description: The sequence length to use for the model.
seq_len.title: Seq Len

attn.description: The attention implementation to use. When CP is enabled, ring attention uses the matching kernel family (FA2 for flash_attention_2, FA3 for flash_attention_3).
attn.title: Attn

compile.description: Whether to compile the model using `torch.compile`.

ac.description: Whether to apply activation checkpointing to the model. If None, will not apply activation checkpointing.

ac_offloading.description: Whether to apply activation offloading to the model. If None, will not apply activation offloading.

fsdp_cpu_offload.description: Whether to enable FSDP CPU offloading for parameters, gradients, and optimizer states. When enabled, uses pinned memory for efficient CPU-GPU transfers.
fsdp_cpu_offload.title: Fsdp Cpu Offload

optim_cpu_offload.description: Whether to enable optimizer state CPU offloading. Unlike fsdp_cpu_offload, this only moves optimizer states (momentum, variance) to CPU, keeping weights on GPU. This avoids the H2D all-gather overhead while still saving GPU memory.
optim_cpu_offload.title: Optim Cpu Offload

reshard_after_forward.description: Whether to reshard the model after each forward pass.
reshard_after_forward.title: Reshard After Forward

dp_replicate.description: The data parallel dim where model weights are replicated.
dp_replicate.title: Dp Replicate

ep.description: The expert parallelism to use if the model has MoE layers. If 1, then no EP will be used.
ep.title: Ep

tp.description: The tensor parallelism size to use. If 1, then no TP will be used.
tp.title: Tp

cp.description: The context parallelism size to use. If 1, then no CP will be used.
cp.title: Cp

impl.description: Model implementation to use. 'auto' (default) selects 'custom' if supported by the model, otherwise 'hf'.
impl.title: Impl

optimization_dtype.description: The dtype to use for the model optimization.
optimization_dtype.title: Optimization Dtype

reduce_dtype.description: The dtype to use for the model reduce.
reduce_dtype.title: Reduce Dtype

moe_use_grouped_mm.description: Whether to use grouped mm for the MoE layers. Require compute capability >= 9.0
moe_use_grouped_mm.title: Moe Use Grouped Mm

freeze_moe_router.description: Whether to freeze the MoE router parameters during training.
freeze_moe_router.title: Freeze Moe Router

lora.description: Whether to apply LoRA to the model. If None, will not apply LoRA.

fused_lm_head_token_chunk_size.description: The flattened token chunk size to use for the fused LM head. Three behaviors: (1) int >= 1: explicitly set the number of tokens per LM-head chunk; (2) 'auto': auto-enable (RL training auto-sets to 8192); (3) 'disabled': explicitly disable fused LM head (use vanilla). Explicitly setting an integer value for this feature isn't supported for SFT training.
fused_lm_head_token_chunk_size.title: Fused Lm Head Token Chunk Size

host.description: The host to use for the NCCL broadcast.
host.title: Host

port.description: The port to use for the NCCL broadcast.
port.title: Port

timeout.description: The timeout in seconds to use for the NCCL broadcast.
timeout.title: Timeout

inference_world_size.description: The number of GPUs used for inference.
inference_world_size.title: Inference World Size

quantize_in_weight_transfer.description: Use kernel-format FP8 quantized NCCL transfer for weight updates. When disabled, uses default HF checkpoint-format transfer.
quantize_in_weight_transfer.title: Quantize In Weight Transfer

inference.description: The inference config. If None, the rl entrypoint will not start an inference server (useful for elastic inference pools or manually started servers).

teacher_inference.description: Teacher inference config. If None, will use the same config as inference or a default config. Only used when teacher GPUs or nodes are set.

output_dir.description: The directory to store the outputs. Should be set to a unique directory identifying the experiment.
output_dir.title: Output Dir

clean_output_dir.description: If true, delete the output directory before starting training. Required to overwrite an output directory that contains checkpoints from a previous run when not resuming.
clean_output_dir.title: Clean Output Dir

log.description: Shared log configs. If None, will fallback to the log configs specified on submodule configs.

ckpt.description: Shared checkpoint configs. If None, will fallback to the checkpoint configs specified on submodule configs.

wandb.description: Shared W&B configs. If None, will fallback to the W&B configs specified on submodule configs.

model.description: Shared model configs. If None, will fallback to the model configs specified on submodule configs.

max_steps.description: The maximum number of steps to train for. If None, will fallback to the max steps specified on submodule configs.
max_steps.title: Max Steps

max_model_len.description: The maximum model length to use. If None, will fallback to the max model length specified on submodule configs.
max_model_len.title: Max Model Len

seq_len.description: Shared sequence length. Propagates to trainer.model.seq_len and orchestrator.seq_len, but only for those not explicitly set in the config. Explicitly set per-component values always take precedence.
seq_len.title: Seq Len

max_async_level.description: The async level to use. If None, will fallback to the async level specified on submodule configs.
max_async_level.title: Max Async Level

bench.description: Whether to run in benchmark mode. Automatically sets the trainer and orchestrator to benchmark mode and, if present, suffixes the W&B project with `-bench`.
bench.title: Bench

slurm.description: SLURM configuration. If None, will run locally.

dry_run.description: Only validate and dump resolved configs and exit early.
dry_run.title: Dry Run
